682	static void add_module_pb_bus_mux_interc(
683	  ModuleManager& module_manager, const ModuleId& pb_module,
684	  std::vector<ModuleId>& memory_modules, std::vector<size_t>& memory_instances,
685	  const CircuitLibrary& circuit_lib, const CircuitModelId& interc_circuit_model,
686	  const CircuitPortId& interc_model_input,
687	  const CircuitPortId& interc_model_output,
688	  const std::vector<t_pb_graph_pin*>& bus_pins, t_interconnect* cur_interc,
689	  const size_t& fan_in, const bool& group_config_block, const bool& verbose) {
690	  VTR_ASSERT(false == bus_pins.empty());
691	  /* The representative pin (lowest bit index) names the shared resources */
692	  t_pb_graph_pin* rep_pb_graph_pin = bus_pins.front();
693	
694	  VTR_ASSERT(CIRCUIT_MODEL_MUX == circuit_lib.model_type(interc_circuit_model));
695	  VTR_ASSERT((2 == fan_in) || (2 < fan_in));
696	
697	  /* Instantiate a single shared memory module for the whole bus, named after
698	   * the representative pin */
699	  auto [mux_mem_module, mux_mem_instance] = add_module_pb_mux_memory(
700	    module_manager, pb_module, memory_modules, memory_instances, circuit_lib,
701	    interc_circuit_model, rep_pb_graph_pin, fan_in, group_config_block,
702	    verbose);
703	
704	  /* Instantiate one mux per bus bit and collect their instance ids */
705	  std::vector<size_t> mux_instances;
706	  for (t_pb_graph_pin* des_pb_graph_pin : bus_pins) {
707	    /* Every bit of the bus must have the same fan-in (number of input ports) */
708	    VTR_ASSERT(fan_in ==
709	               pb_graph_pin_inputs(des_pb_graph_pin, cur_interc).size());
710	    mux_instances.push_back(add_module_pb_graph_pin_mux_instance(
711	      module_manager, pb_module, circuit_lib, interc_circuit_model,
712	      interc_model_input, interc_model_output, des_pb_graph_pin, cur_interc,
713	      fan_in));
714	  }
715	
716	  VTR_LOGV(verbose,
717	           "Built bus-based mux '%s' with %lu shared bit-muxes driven by a "
718	           "single selector\n",
719	           cur_interc->name, mux_instances.size());
720	
721	  /* Wire the shared memory output to the SRAM ports of every mux instance */
722	  ModuleId mux_module = module_manager.find_module(generate_mux_subckt_name(
723	    circuit_lib, interc_circuit_model, fan_in, std::string()));
724	  add_module_nets_between_logics_and_shared_memory_sram_bus(
725	    module_manager, pb_module, mux_module, mux_instances, mux_mem_module,
726	    mux_mem_instance, circuit_lib, interc_circuit_model);
727	}
728	
729	/********************************************************************
730	 * We check output_pins of cur_pb_graph_node and its the input_edges
731	 * Built the interconnections between outputs of cur_pb_graph_node and outputs
732	 *of child_pb_graph_node src_pb_graph_node.[in|out]_pins ----------------->
733	 *des_pb_graph_node.[in|out]pins
734	 *                                        /|\
735	 *                                         |
736	 *                         input_pins,   edges,       output_pins
737	 *
738	 * This function does the following task:
739	 * 1. identify pin interconnection type,
740	 * 2. Identify the number of fan-in (Consider interconnection edges of only
741	 *selected mode)
742	 * 3. Add mux/direct connection as a child module to pb_module
743	 * 4. Add nets related to the mux/direction
744	 *******************************************************************/
745	static void add_module_pb_graph_pin_interc(
746	  ModuleManager& module_manager, const ModuleId& pb_module,
747	  std::vector<ModuleId>& memory_modules, std::vector<size_t>& memory_instances,
748	  const VprDeviceAnnotation& device_annotation,
749	  const CircuitLibrary& circuit_lib, t_pb_graph_pin* des_pb_graph_pin,
750	  t_mode* physical_mode, const bool& group_config_block, const bool& verbose) {
751	  /* Find the number of fan-in and detailed interconnection information
752	   * related to the destination pb_graph_pin
753	   */
754	  t_interconnect* cur_interc =
755	    pb_graph_pin_interc(des_pb_graph_pin, physical_mode);
756	  size_t fan_in = pb_graph_pin_inputs(des_pb_graph_pin, cur_interc).size();
757	
758	  /* If no interconnection is needed, we can return early */
759	  if ((nullptr == cur_interc) || (0 == fan_in)) {
760	    return;
761	  }
762	
763	  /* Initialize the interconnection type that will be physically implemented in
764	   * module */
765	  enum e_interconnect interc_type =
766	    device_annotation.interconnect_physical_type(cur_interc);
767	  const CircuitModelId& interc_circuit_model =
768	    device_annotation.interconnect_circuit_model(cur_interc);
769	
770	  /* Find input ports of the wire module */
771	  std::vector<CircuitPortId> interc_model_inputs =
772	    circuit_lib.model_ports_by_type(
773	      interc_circuit_model, CIRCUIT_MODEL_PORT_INPUT,
774	      true); /* the last argument to guarantee that we ignore any global inputs
775	              */
776	  /* Find output ports of the wire module */
777	  std::vector<CircuitPortId> interc_model_outputs =
778	    circuit_lib.model_ports_by_type(interc_circuit_model,
779	                                    CIRCUIT_MODEL_PORT_OUTPUT,
780	                                    true); /* the last argument to guarantee
781	                                              that we ignore any global ports */
782	
783	  /* Ensure that we have only 1 input port and 1 output port, this is valid for
784	   * both wire and MUX */
785	  VTR_ASSERT(1 == interc_model_inputs.size());
786	  VTR_ASSERT(1 == interc_model_outputs.size());
787	
788	  /* Special handling for bus-based muxes: all the bits of the output bus share
789	   * a single selector. We process the whole bus once, from its representative
790	   * (lowest-index) pin, and skip the remaining bits.
791	   * Note: the 'bus' attribute is only valid on <mux> tags (MUX_INTERC) in the
792	   * VPR architecture, so this never applies to crossbars or direct wires. */
793	  if ((true == cur_interc->bus) && (MUX_INTERC == interc_type)) {
794	    std::vector<t_pb_graph_pin*> bus_pins =
795	      pb_graph_interc_sink_pins(des_pb_graph_pin, cur_interc, physical_mode);
796	    VTR_ASSERT(false == bus_pins.empty());
797	    /* Only the representative pin builds the whole bus group */
798	    if (des_pb_graph_pin != bus_pins.front()) {
799	      return;
800	    }
801	    add_module_pb_bus_mux_interc(
802	      module_manager, pb_module, memory_modules, memory_instances, circuit_lib,
803	      interc_circuit_model, interc_model_inputs[0], interc_model_outputs[0],
804	      bus_pins, cur_interc, fan_in, group_config_block, verbose);
805	    return;
806	  }
807	
808	  /* Branch on the type of physical implementation,
809	   * We add instances of programmable interconnection
810	   */
811	  switch (interc_type) {

107	static void build_switch_block_mux_module(
108	  ModuleManager& module_manager, const ModuleId& sb_module,
109	  const VprDeviceAnnotation& device_annotation, const DeviceGrid& grids,
110	  const RRGraphView& rr_graph, const RRGSB& rr_gsb,
111	  const CircuitLibrary& circuit_lib, const e_side& chan_side,
112	  const size_t& chan_node_id, const RRNodeId& cur_rr_node,
113	  const std::vector<RRNodeId>& driver_rr_nodes, const RRSwitchId& switch_index,
114	  const std::map<ModulePinInfo, ModuleNetId>& input_port_to_module_nets,
115	  const bool& group_config_block) {
116	  /* Check current rr_node is CHANX or CHANY*/
117	  VTR_ASSERT((e_rr_type::CHANX == rr_graph.node_type(cur_rr_node)) ||
118	             (e_rr_type::CHANY == rr_graph.node_type(cur_rr_node)));
119	
120	  /* Get the circuit model id of the routing multiplexer */
121	  CircuitModelId mux_model =
122	    device_annotation.rr_switch_circuit_model(switch_index);
123	
124	  /* Find the input size of the implementation of a routing multiplexer */
125	  size_t datapath_mux_size = driver_rr_nodes.size();
126	
127	  /* Find the module name of the multiplexer and try to find it in the module
128	   * manager */
129	  std::string mux_module_name = generate_mux_subckt_name(
130	    circuit_lib, mux_model, datapath_mux_size, std::string(""));
131	  ModuleId mux_module = module_manager.find_module(mux_module_name);
132	  VTR_ASSERT(true == module_manager.valid_module_id(mux_module));
133	
134	  /* Get the MUX instance id from the module manager */
135	  size_t mux_instance_id = module_manager.num_instance(sb_module, mux_module);
136	  /* Instanciate the MUX Module */

content.content:
=== busmux task.conf ===
# = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = =
# Configuration file for running experiments
# = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = =
# This task exercises bus-based multiplexer (<mux bus="true"/>) support.
# The architecture is identical to k4n4_frac_mult except that the
# mult_32x32_slice 'a2a' interconnect is a 32-bit-wide bus mux. In OpenFPGA the
# 32 single-bit muxes that VPR expands the bus mux into must share a single
# configuration memory (one shared config bit) instead of 32 separate ones.
# = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = = =

[GENERAL]
run_engine=openfpga_shell
power_tech_file = ${PATH:OPENFPGA_PATH}/openfpga_flow/tech/PTM_45nm/45nm.xml
